suites.name: mt-tool-exec.arm-f-param-only
suites.title: Arm (f) param-only two-pass (grammar-pinned name + args)
suites.description: Per step: pass 1 grammar-pins tool-name choice from a closed enum; pass 2 grammar-pins args for that exact tool. The model never makes both decisions in the same call. Tests whether wrong-name + wrong-arg-name is the failure mode that scaffold can address; if it is, accuracy lifts and the bottleneck moves elsewhere.

suites.name: mt-tool-exec.arm-f-param-only-1pass
suites.title: Arm (f-1pass) param-only single-call (grammar-pinned tool_use)
suites.description: Same hypothesis as arm (f) but one model call per step instead of two: a oneOf grammar pins both name (const) and args (strict schema) per branch. Comparator for arm (f) — measures whether the second pass earns its round-trip.

data.answer:

BEGIN:

```powershell
Add-Member -MemberType NoteToConsole -Name "Files" -Value @(Get-Content -Path 'part1.txt') –Value@(Get-Content -Path 'part2.txt') | Format-Table, -AutoSize
Invoke-Command -ComputerName workstation -ScriptBlock {
    $inputData = Get-Content -Path ("$PSScriptRoot\files.csv")
    $outputData = [xml]$inputData
    $outputData.Files | ForEach-Object { $_.AppendChild($_.InnerHtml) }
    Write-Host "Files have been changed to HTML!"
}

Write-Host "The output is ready."
```

END:

  - **Write-Host "Files have been changed to HTML!"**: Displays a summary message.
  
- **Final Check**: After execution, confirm that two files are present with the merged contents.
